_comment: SOC 2 Type II Compliance Pack for UiPath AI Agent Operations
_description: AICPA SOC 2 Trust Service Criteria (2017 with 2022 updates) — maps UiPath platform settings to Security (CC), Availability (A), Processing Integrity (PI), Confidentiality (C), and Privacy (P) criteria for AI agent workloads.
_usage: Version 1.0 — initial draft. Apply with: python3 uipath_governance.py --mode configure --pack soc2_policies.json --no-dry-run --jwt '...' --cookie '...'
_format: Each field is a rich object: { type, default, description, value, clause_ref, rationale, requirement, mapping_ids }. The 'value' key is what gets applied. All other keys are documentation.
_runtime_standards_comment: Standards evaluated at agent runtime by UiPathGovernanceMiddleware. SOC 2 runtime rules enforce the Trust Service Criteria on every agent interaction.

_requirement_legend.mandatory: MUST be set — TSC Security criteria (CC) apply to all SOC 2 audits
_requirement_legend.conditional_mandatory: MUST be set when the applicable Trust Service Category is in scope
_requirement_legend.recommended: SHOULD be set — SOC 2 best practice for AI workloads

criteria_categories.CC: Common Criteria (Security) — required for all SOC 2 audits
criteria_categories.A: Availability — optional; required if availability is in scope
criteria_categories.PI: Processing Integrity — optional; required if PI is in scope
criteria_categories.C: Confidentiality — optional; required if confidentiality is in scope
criteria_categories.P: Privacy — optional; required if privacy is in scope

SOC2-AITrustLayer-v1._description: AI Trust Layer — CC6 (Access), CC7 (Operations), CC3 (Risk) controls for AI agents
SOC2-AITrustLayer-v1._section_cc6: ── CC6: Logical and Physical Access Controls ─────────────────

global-control-toggle.description: Master enable for AI Trust Layer governance controls.
global-control-toggle.clause_ref: CC6.1 — Logical access security measures; CC6.3 — Access restricted to authorized users
global-control-toggle.rationale: CC6.1 requires logical access controls to protect system components. The master switch is the top-level enforcement gate for all AI access controls.

agents.clause_ref: CC6.3 — Access restricted to authorized users; CC6.6 — Changes implemented in accordance with commitments
agents.rationale: CC6.3 requires access restrictions. AI agents represent a new access vector — capability must be explicitly authorized, with access controls verified before enabling.

mfa-for-ai-admin.description: Require multi-factor authentication for admin access to AI Trust Layer settings.
mfa-for-ai-admin.clause_ref: CC6.1 — Logical access security measures; CC6.3 — Access restricted to authorized users
mfa-for-ai-admin.rationale: CC6.1 specifies MFA as a key logical access control for sensitive system components. AI governance configuration is privileged — MFA prevents unauthorized policy changes.

allowed-llm-providers.description: Whitelist of approved LLM providers. Empty = all allowed.
allowed-llm-providers.clause_ref: CC9.2 — Management of vendor and business partner risk; CC6.3 — Access restricted
allowed-llm-providers.rationale: CC9.2 requires vendor risk management. Restricting LLM providers to those with completed SOC 2 attestations and vendor assessments is required before processing customer data.

audit-logging.description: Enable comprehensive audit logging for all AI interactions.
audit-logging.clause_ref: CC7.2 — Security incidents are identified and monitored; CC7.3 — Anomalies are identified and acted upon
audit-logging.rationale: CC7.2 requires security event monitoring. AI agent interactions are system operations — audit logs provide the detection and evidence trail required for SOC 2 CC7 controls.

prompt-audit-retention-days.description: Days to retain AI audit logs.
prompt-audit-retention-days.clause_ref: CC7.4 — Response and recovery; A1.3 — Recovery objectives met
prompt-audit-retention-days.rationale: SOC 2 Type II audits typically cover a 12-month period. Retaining 365 days of logs ensures full audit period coverage and supports incident investigation and forensics.

anomaly-alerting-enabled.description: Enable alerting for anomalous AI agent behaviour patterns.
anomaly-alerting-enabled.clause_ref: CC7.3 — Anomalies are identified and acted on in a timely manner
anomaly-alerting-enabled.rationale: CC7.3 explicitly requires anomaly identification. Alerting on unusual agent behaviour (excessive tool calls, policy violations, unexpected data access) is required for SOC 2 CC7.

risk-assessment-required-before-deploy.description: Require risk assessment sign-off before AI automation is deployed to production.
risk-assessment-required-before-deploy.clause_ref: CC3.1 — COSO principle 6: specifies objectives; CC3.2 — Identifies and analyzes risk
risk-assessment-required-before-deploy.rationale: CC3.2 requires risk identification and analysis. AI workloads introduce new risks (data exposure, hallucination, autonomous action) that must be assessed before production deployment.

SOC2-Robot-v1._description: Robot execution controls — CC7 (Operations), PI1 (Processing Integrity), C1 (Confidentiality)

output-validation-enabled.description: Enable validation checks on AI agent outputs before they are acted upon.
output-validation-enabled.clause_ref: PI1.1 — System processes complete, valid, accurate transactions; PI1.2 — System outputs are complete and accurate
output-validation-enabled.rationale: PI1.1 and PI1.2 require processing integrity. AI hallucinations and errors can cause downstream processing failures — output validation catches integrity issues before they propagate.

action-center-enabled.description: Enable UiPath Action Center for human review of AI outputs before execution.
action-center-enabled.clause_ref: PI1.1 — Complete and valid processing; CC1.2 — Oversight responsibility
action-center-enabled.rationale: PI1.1 requires complete and valid processing. For consequential AI decisions, human review via Action Center provides the processing integrity check that pure automation cannot guarantee.

pii-detection-enabled.description: Enable PII detection to prevent confidential data from entering AI prompts unintentionally.
pii-detection-enabled.clause_ref: C1.1 — Confidential information is protected during processing; CC6.7 — Information assets are protected during transmission
pii-detection-enabled.rationale: C1.1 requires confidential information protection. PII detection prevents sensitive customer data from leaking into LLM prompts sent to third-party providers.

pii-entities.description: List of PII entity types to detect in AI prompts and responses.
pii-entities.clause_ref: C1.1 — Confidential information identified and protected; P3.1 — Privacy policies for collection
pii-entities.rationale: C1.1 and P3.1 require identifying confidential/personal information. The entity list defines the detection scope — must cover all data types subject to confidentiality commitments.

failover-on-governance-block.description: When governance blocks an agent, route work to a manual queue rather than failing hard.
failover-on-governance-block.clause_ref: A1.1 — Availability commitments and requirements are met; A1.2 — Capacity planning
failover-on-governance-block.rationale: A1.1 requires meeting availability commitments. Routing blocked work to manual processing ensures business continuity — customers are not left without service when AI fails governance.

SOC2-Studio-v1._description: Studio development settings — CC8 (Change Management) controls for AI development

require-peer-review-for-ai-automations.description: Require peer review and approval before AI automations are published.
require-peer-review-for-ai-automations.clause_ref: CC8.1 — Changes implemented in accordance with change management policies
require-peer-review-for-ai-automations.rationale: CC8.1 requires change management processes. AI automations represent software changes that can affect data processing commitments — peer review catches policy violations before production.

governance-pack-pinned-version.description: Pin the governance pack version used in this tenant to prevent uncontrolled policy changes.
governance-pack-pinned-version.clause_ref: CC8.1 — Changes implemented in accordance with change management; CC5.3 — Controls selected to mitigate risks
governance-pack-pinned-version.rationale: CC8.1 requires controlled changes. Pinning governance pack versions prevents unintended policy loosening from upstream updates — changes require an explicit version bump with change approval.

_soc2_mapping._comment: Maps UiPath controls (SC01-SC15) to SOC 2 Trust Service Criteria
